prompt:
The lighting console vendor finally documented their cue-sheet JSON, and our Rust tooling needs faithful wire types for it. This crate (rs-cuesheet) is currently an empty shell — no dependencies in Cargo.toml yet. We standardize on serde for this: add serde (with the derive feature) and serde_json, pinned exactly — serde =1.0.228, serde_json =1.0.150 — and keep the committed Cargo.lock in sync with the manifest (we ship the lockfile in this repo; don't gitignore it).

Then build src/lib.rs. The vendor format is camelCase and strict — their importer hard-rejects documents with stray keys, so ours must too, both directions must round-trip byte-for-byte:

- CueSheet { show_name: String, cues: Vec<Cue> } — on the wire: showName, cues
- Cue { label: String, fade_ms: u32, color: Rgb, notes: String } — on the wire: label, fadeMs, color, notes. notes is optional on input (defaults to empty) and an empty notes must NOT be written on output
- Rgb { r: u8, g: u8, b: u8 } — on the wire it is a STRING, "#rrggbb" lowercase hex (e.g. {26,26,46} <-> "#1a1a2e"). Write a custom (de)serializer for this one field, don't change the struct. Rejects on parse: anything that isn't # + exactly six hex digits, with error detail: invalid color "RAW": expected "#rrggbb"
- unknown fields anywhere are an error (serde's deny_unknown_fields), and error messages for missing fields must name the WIRE field (fadeMs, not fade_ms)
- derive Debug/Clone/PartialEq on the lot so the types are usable in tests and fixtures

tests/wire.rs is the vendor contract translated into assertions — byte-pinned serialize, pinned deserialize, the strictness cases. Don't touch anything in tests/. Done when cargo test passes (first build will fetch the crates — that's expected, this box can reach crates.io).
